work.title: Case Studies
work.subtitle: Deep dives into real projects—problems, approaches, and outcomes.

psol.title: PSOL Protocol
psol.problem: Users needed a privacy layer for Solana transactions without compromising on speed or security.
psol.approach: Developed a zero-knowledge privacy protocol enabling anonymous transactions on Solana with minimal latency.
psol.outcome: Launched as the leading privacy protocol on Solana with thousands of private transactions processed.

humanRail.title: Human Rail
humanRail.problem: Web3 lacked a decentralized identity solution that could verify real humans while preserving privacy.
humanRail.approach: Built an on-chain identity protocol using Soulbound tokens and verifiable credentials on Solana.
humanRail.outcome: Successfully deployed identity verification system used by multiple dApps for sybil resistance.

maniaAtelier.title: Mania Atelier
maniaAtelier.problem: Fashion brand needed a modern, high-performance online store for men and women clothing.
maniaAtelier.approach: Designed a sleek e-commerce platform with seamless checkout, inventory management, and mobile-first UX.
maniaAtelier.outcome: Increased online sales by 150% and achieved sub-2-second page load times across all devices.

trailblazer.title: Trailblazer Agent
trailblazer.problem: Crypto community needed an AI agent to track and analyze emerging narratives on Solana.
trailblazer.approach: Created an autonomous AI agent that scans social media, forums, and on-chain data to identify trends.
trailblazer.outcome: Became a go-to narrative hunter for Solana traders, identifying trends before they went viral.

solAudit.title: SOL Audit
solAudit.problem: Developers needed automated security analysis and fix proposals for Solana GitHub repositories.
solAudit.approach: Built an AI agent that scans Solana-native GitHub repos, identifies vulnerabilities, proposes fixes, and auto-forks with improvements.
solAudit.outcome: Analyzed 500+ repositories, proposed 2000+ security fixes, and auto-forked critical projects with enhanced security.

swarpFoundation.title: Swarp Foundation
swarpFoundation.problem: Swarp Foundation needed an official web presence to showcase their mission and ecosystem initiatives.
swarpFoundation.approach: Designed and developed a professional, accessible website with governance features and documentation.
swarpFoundation.outcome: Established the foundation's digital identity with integrated governance and community engagement tools.

splitguard.title: SplitGuard
splitguard.problem: Solana projects needed a native solution for transparent and automated fee sharing among stakeholders.
splitguard.approach: Developed a smart contract platform enabling programmable fee distribution with customizable splits.
splitguard.outcome: Enabled multiple projects to distribute over $1M in shared fees transparently and efficiently.

liveDrops.title: Live Drops
liveDrops.problem: Creators needed a platform to launch tokens and NFTs with live streaming capabilities for engagement.
liveDrops.approach: Built a stream launch platform integrating real-time video with token launches and live minting.
liveDrops.outcome: Hosted 100+ successful launches with live community engagement and instant liquidity provision.

simfi.title: SimFi
simfi.problem: Traders wanted to practice memecoin trading strategies without risking real capital.
simfi.approach: Created a paper trading platform with real-time Solana memecoin prices and simulated portfolios.
simfi.outcome: Attracted thousands of users practicing strategies before deploying real capital to live markets.

funpumpfun.title: FunPumpFun
funpumpfun.problem: Users wanted a gamified way to learn about token launches without financial risk.
funpumpfun.approach: Built an engaging simulation game replicating the Pump.fun experience with virtual currency.
funpumpfun.outcome: Became a popular educational tool and game, with users spending hours mastering launch mechanics.

noCrying.title: No Crying
noCrying.problem: A growing memecoin community needed a dedicated fan page with real-time updates and engagement tools.
noCrying.approach: Created a vibrant community hub with price tracking, meme gallery, and social features.
noCrying.outcome: Built a thriving community hub with thousands of daily active users and viral content sharing.

scratchNSol.title: Scratch n SOL
scratchNSol.problem: Solana users wanted a fun, on-chain scratch card game with instant prizes and provable fairness.
scratchNSol.approach: Developed a blockchain-based scratch and win game with verifiable randomness and instant payouts.
scratchNSol.outcome: Processed thousands of games daily with instant SOL payouts and provably fair outcomes.

desertRoseGin.title: Desert Rose Gin
desertRoseGin.problem: A premium gin brand needed an elegant digital presence to showcase their craft spirits and drive online sales.
desertRoseGin.approach: Designed a sophisticated brand website with immersive storytelling, Shopify integration for direct sales, and smooth animations that reflect the premium nature of the product.
desertRoseGin.outcome: Established a strong online brand identity with seamless shopping experience, resulting in increased direct-to-consumer sales and brand recognition.

mindset.title: Security-First Mindset
items: Threat modeling for every blockchain project

faq.title: Common Questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to remove blockers. Have more? Let's talk.

q1.question: What do you need from me to start?
q1.answer: A clear problem statement, any existing documentation or code, and access to stakeholders for discovery calls. The more context you provide upfront, the faster we can move. Ideally, come with: user stories, wireframes (if applicable), and a rough timeline.

q2.question: How do pricing and milestones work?
q2.answer: I typically work with milestone-based payments tied to deliverables. For example: 30% upfront, 40% at midpoint demo, 30% at final delivery. This aligns incentives and reduces risk for both parties.

q3.question: Do you do fixed-price or hourly?
q3.answer: I prefer fixed-price for well-defined scopes (like the MVP Sprint) and hourly/retainer for ongoing or exploratory work. We'll discuss what makes sense for your project during the discovery call.

q4.question: Can you audit existing code?
q4.answer: Yes. I offer security reviews and code audits for smart contracts and Web3 applications. This includes threat modeling, vulnerability assessment, and a detailed report with prioritized fixes.

q5.question: Do you provide ongoing maintenance?
q5.answer: Yes, through monthly retainer agreements. This includes monitoring, bug fixes, performance optimization, and feature iterations. SLAs available for critical systems.

q6.question: What chains/stacks do you work with?
q6.answer: Primary: Solana (Rust/Anchor), Ethereum (Solidity/Foundry), Next.js/React, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, Kubernetes. I'm also comfortable learning new stacks if your project requires it.

q7.question: How do you handle timezones?
q7.answer: I'm based in PST (UTC-8) and have worked with teams across US, EU, and APAC. I adjust my schedule for important meetings and use async communication (Notion, Slack, Loom) to keep things moving.

q8.question: What's your typical response time?
q8.answer: For active projects: same day for Slack, within 24 hours for email. For urgent issues (production outages), I'm available via phone/Signal.
